page: Google Cloud Platform ct6e-standard-8t is an accelerator-optimized server instance designed for large-scale machine learning and parallel computing workloads. It features an x86_64 architecture with 360 dedicated vCPUs and 1440.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 8 integrated Google V6 lite TPUs (ct6e accelerators) to handle intensive tensor processing tasks.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile represents a highly specialized resource density, trading local storage for massive parallel compute and TPU acceleration, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for distributed deep learning training and large-scale inference workloads that would otherwise require multiple smaller nodes.

The ct6e-standard-8t is engineered primarily for workloads that can leverage Google TPU hardware, such as training large language models, executing high-throughput neural network inference, and running complex data science pipelines. Its massive 360 vCPU count also makes it suitable for heavy preprocessing tasks that precede machine learning training phases.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct6e-standard-8t is an accelerator-optimized server instance designed for large-scale machine learning and parallel computing. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 360 dedicated vCPUs and 1440.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 8 Google V6 lite TPUs (ct6e accelerators) to accelerate tensor-based computations. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, relying instead on network-attached storage. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as deep learning model training, high-throughput neural network inference, and large-scale data science pipelines.
